items.final_wording: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
source.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
source.setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

items.rationale: The exact melanoma assessment section supports recording specialist mole or pigmented-lesion review, dermoscopy, baseline photography and interval comparison without diagnosing melanoma or selecting excision.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation

source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.

items.rationale: The follow-up section supports documenting clinician-led full skin examination, reviewed prior specialist records, patient concerns, safety net and an arranged follow-up; it does not generate a surveillance interval or investigation.
